title: Stability and Periodicity in Modular Representation Theory
speaker: Nate Harman
abstract: I will review certain stabilization phenomena in the characteristic zero representation theory of general linear and symmetric groups as the rank tends to infinity. Then I will give a survey of some results and conjectures about analogs of these in the positive characteristic and quantum settings, exploring what happens as the rank tends to infinity while the characteristic either remains fixed or grows at a rate comparable to the rank.
tags: Virtual Workshop on Recent Developments in Geometric Representation Theory

proof_stages.label: Representation stability as rank tends to infinity
proof_stages.summary: The talk frames stability as the phenomenon that the representation theory of a rank-indexed family of groups or algebras eventually stops depending on the rank, or depends on it only in a controlled polynomial way.

proof_stages.label: Characteristic-zero prototypes: GL_n and symmetric groups
proof_stages.summary: Classical Schur-Weyl and symmetric-group examples show how stable labeling by partitions makes multiplicities independent of n and dimensions polynomial in n, including stable Kronecker and Littlewood-Richardson behavior.

proof_stages.label: FI-modules as a structural mechanism for stability
proof_stages.summary: FI-modules are introduced as functors from finite sets and injections, with finite generation in characteristic zero forcing representation stability and explaining many natural examples such as configuration-space cohomology.

proof_stages.label: Positive-characteristic GL_n: stability plus modular periodicity
proof_stages.summary: In characteristic p, polynomial GL_n representations still stabilize, but algebraic representations such as the adjoint representation reveal dependence on n modulo p and hence motivate periodic rather than purely stable behavior.

proof_stages.label: Modular symmetric-group periodicity and FI-module consequences
proof_stages.summary: For symmetric groups in characteristic p, truncating by complexity gives equivalences between categories for ranks differing by a large enough p-power, yielding periodic decomposition numbers, PORC dimensions, and a mixed periodic/stable theory for finitely generated FI-modules.

proof_stages.label: Cross-characteristic stability through limiting categories
proof_stages.summary: When n and p both tend to infinity with n congruent to a fixed integer modulo p, decomposition numbers again stabilize, a fact explained categorically via a model-theoretic limiting relation with Deligne-type abelian envelopes.

proof_stages.label: Extensions, analogues, and open directions across other families
proof_stages.summary: The final part surveys known, expected, and mysterious analogues for algebraic GL_n, Hecke and q-Schur algebras, finite groups of Lie type, braid and arithmetic groups, and related geometric or categorical frameworks.
